ui.title: Course Placement Assessment
ui.subtitle: This test helps determine where the learner should start, which roadmap fits best, and whether preparation is needed before the main course.
ui.noQuestions: There are no placement questions for this course yet.
ui.question: Question {{index}}

pythonFoundation.title: Python Foundation
pythonFoundation.desc: Placement assessment for the Python from zero course.

ready.title: Ready to start the main course
ready.desc: The learner can start the main course and take additional practice exercises.
ready.nextStep: Start Python Foundation from lesson 1 and introduce small projects early.

foundation.title: Start from the foundation section
foundation.desc: The learner should follow a slower roadmap with examples and step-by-step practice.
foundation.nextStep: Start Python Foundation at a slower pace and check progress every 3-4 lessons.

preparation.title: Strengthen thinking skills before coding
preparation.desc: The learner should strengthen logical thinking, input/output, and conditions before entering the main course.
preparation.nextStep: Assign thinking tests, logic exercises, and introductory lessons before Python.

placePy001.question: In programming, what is data given to a program usually called?
placePy001.explain: Input is data provided to a program for processing.

placePy002.question: In programming, what is a variable usually used for?
placePy002.explain: A variable stores data so the program can use it.

placePy003.question: If a score of 5 or above means passing, and a learner scores 6, what is the result?
placePy003.explain: 6 is greater than 5, so the passing condition is met.

placePy004.question: If you need to print 'Hello' 10 times, what programming idea fits best?

options.D: Use a loop to repeat the action

placePy004.explain: A loop repeats an action multiple times without writing the same code again and again.

placePy005.question: A user enters their age from the keyboard. If you want to compare the age with the number 18, what should you pay attention to?

options.B: The input may need to be converted to a number if it starts as text
options.D: Only the text color needs changing

placePy005.explain: Keyboard input is often text. To do numeric comparison, it must be converted to a number.

placePy006.question: If you want to store the names of 30 learners in a program, which structure is most suitable?

options.B: One variable storing only one name

placePy006.explain: A list is suitable for storing many values of the same kind, such as learner names.

placePy007.question: If the same processing logic is used many times in a program, what is the best way to organize it?
placePy007.explain: Functions help break a program into reusable pieces of logic.

placePy008.question: A program runs but gives the wrong result. What is the most suitable way to check it?

options.D: Check the values after each processing step

placePy008.explain: Checking values step by step helps identify where the error appears.

placePy009.question: A program that calculates an order total should process steps in which order?

options.A: Print invoice → enter products → calculate total
options.B: Enter products → calculate total → apply discount → show result

placePy009.explain: Product data is needed first, then the total is calculated, discounts are applied, and the result is displayed.

placePy010.question: When facing a large programming problem, what is the best approach?

options.A: Break it into smaller manageable parts
options.D: Only build the interface and skip the logic

placePy010.explain: Breaking down a problem makes it easier to understand, implement, test, and fix.

placePy011.question: A working adult wants to process an employee list file with Python. What is usually the first step?

options.C: Read data from the file into the program

placePy011.explain: To process data in a file, the program usually needs to read the file first.

placePy012.question: A task must be repeated every day, such as renaming many files. Why is Python useful here?

options.B: It is only used for drawing
options.D: It can automate repeated actions based on rules

placePy012.explain: Python is useful for automating repetitive tasks such as file handling, renaming files, and reading or writing data.

placePy013.question: A revenue table has 3 days: 100, 150, 120. Which statement is correct?
placePy013.explain: 150 is the highest value, so Day 2 has the highest revenue.

placePy014.question: If a program does these steps: set x = 2, then x = x + 3, what is the final value of x?
placePy014.explain: Initially x is 2. Then x = x + 3, so the new value of x is 5.

placePy015.question: When learning programming, what is the best reaction after encountering errors many times?

options.C: Read the error, isolate the cause, and fix it step by step

placePy015.explain: Errors are normal in programming. Reading errors, isolating causes, and fixing step by step are important skills.
